heading: Contracts for transportation of Federal Government securities
label: 6308 Contracts for transportation of Federal Government securities
text: When practicable, a contract for transporting bullion, cash, or securities of the Federal Government shall be awarded to the lowest responsible bidder after notice to all parties with means of transportation.
notes: Historical and Revision NotesRevisedSectionSource (U.S. Code)Source (Statutes at Large)630841:24., (words after “fifty five thousand dollars” in 3d par. under heading “Miscellaneous Objects Under the Treasury Department”), .July 7, 1884, ch. 33223 Stat. 204The words “bullion, cash, or securities of the Federal Government” are substituted for “moneys, bullion, coin, notes, bonds, and other securities of the United States, and paper” to eliminate unnecessary words. The word “awarded” is substituted for “let” to use more modern terminology.
